behaviors.description: Traveling-salesman heuristic route length stays within 1.5x of optimal for <=20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in heuristic quality/algorithm logic, not a cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required; deterministic in-memory coordinate sets expose failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI step. Rejection-gate: higher levels were rejected because behavior text is only about heuristic-vs-optimal ratio, with no external service boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function with representative in-memory stop sets (including adversarial layouts), compare produced route length against precomputed/verified optimal baselines, and fail if any case exceeds 1.5x.

behaviors.description: Delivery time windows are computed from inter-stop distance and configured average speed by road type.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is formula/config-selection logic at a local function boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS needed. Rejection-gate: Integration/System rejected because behavior text specifies deterministic inputs (distance, configured speed) and expected arithmetic output, not module wiring failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test the estimator function with fixed distances and road-type speed config values; assert per-leg and total window outputs, including rounding/edge cases (zero distance, missing road type defaults if defined).

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift and returns excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local threshold/excess computation logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no external dependency needed to reproduce. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text is strictly validator decision + computed excess output.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with route-time totals around the boundary (below/equal/above remaining shift) and assert acceptance/rejection plus exact excess duration when above limit.

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (no API results), service skips it, optimizes remaining stops, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module handoff (geocoding result -> filtering -> optimizer input -> reporting), not in one pure function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required because a stub geocoder returning 'no results' reproduces the branch; behavior text does not require transport-level failures. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected by behavior text 'API returns no results' (content-level response), not timeout/socket/protocol behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + geocoder client interface + optimizer + report output in-process; stub one geocode response as empty and others valid; assert skipped-address list contains the failed address and route is built only from successfully geocoded stops.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses are consolidated into one stop with combined items and not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is cross-module data handoff (dedup/merge stage feeding optimizer/route output) rather than only one local function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency. Rejection-gate: Unit rejected because behavior explicitly includes downstream effect 'not visited twice' in produced route, requiring module wiring beyond isolated merge logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the in-process service pipeline with duplicate-address inputs carrying distinct item lists; assert merged stop has combined items and resulting route contains exactly one visit for that address.
